isolation.command: cd agent && npx vitest run src/__tests__/sessions.test.ts src/__tests__/disambiguation-followup.test.ts --reporter=verbose

assertions.title: Follow-up reply attaches to correct task

steps.expected: Tests for findSessionForReply pass, verifying label-based routing

steps.action: Read test source for 'should match by task label when reply contains the label (VAL-SESSION-001)'
steps.expected: Test creates two sessions with different labels, sends reply containing one label, verifies correct session matched
steps.observed: Test creates session1 (get food) and session2 (call mom), calls findSessionForReply('get food at 10pm', [session1, session2]), asserts result equals session1

steps.expected: When only one active session exists, any reply attaches to it
steps.observed: Test 'should return the single active session when only one exists' passes — findSessionForReply('10pm', [session]) returns the session

steps.expected: Reply mentioning second task label matches second session
steps.observed: Test 'should handle the second session matching by label' passes — findSessionForReply('call mom at 6pm', [session1, session2]) returns session2

relevantTests: findSessionForReply > should match by task label when reply contains the label (VAL-SESSION-001) ✓
findSessionForReply > should return the single active session when only one exists ✓
findSessionForReply > should handle the second session matching by label ✓
findSessionForReply > should match case-insensitively by task label ✓

steps.expected: Tests for multiple session coexistence pass

steps.action: Read test source for 'Multiple active sessions coexisting (VAL-SESSION-002)'
steps.expected: Test verifies multiple sessions can exist for the same user and resolving one doesn't affect others
steps.observed: Test 'should allow multiple sessions for the same user' creates 3 sessions for user-1 and verifies getActiveSessions returns all 3. Test 'should not affect one session when resolving another' resolves session-1 and verifies the update targets only session-1 by id.

relevantTests: Multiple active sessions coexisting (VAL-SESSION-002) > should allow multiple sessions for the same user ✓
Multiple active sessions coexisting (VAL-SESSION-002) > should not affect one session when resolving another ✓

assertions.title: Task name mentioned in follow-up prompts

steps.expected: Tests for buildFollowUpPrompt pass, verifying task name inclusion

steps.action: Read test source for buildFollowUpPrompt tests
steps.expected: Tests verify that the prompt contains the task label for every prompt type
steps.observed: Tests verify: (1) awaiting_time prompt contains 'get food', (2) awaiting_date prompt contains 'call mom', (3) generic prompt contains 'buy milk', (4) disambiguation prompt contains 'which task', (5) exhaustive test iterates 4 task labels × 3 prompt types and asserts each prompt contains the label

relevantTests: buildFollowUpPrompt (VAL-SESSION-003) > should include the task label in awaiting_time prompts ✓
buildFollowUpPrompt (VAL-SESSION-003) > should include the task label in awaiting_date prompts ✓
buildFollowUpPrompt (VAL-SESSION-003) > should include the task label in generic prompts ✓
buildFollowUpPrompt (VAL-SESSION-003) > should handle disambiguation prompts ✓
buildFollowUpPrompt (VAL-SESSION-003) > should always include the task name in follow-up prompts for non-disambiguation cases ✓

assertions.title: Ambiguous reply across sessions triggers disambiguation

steps.action: Run vitest on sessions.test.ts and disambiguation-followup.test.ts
steps.expected: Tests for ambiguous reply detection and disambiguation flow pass
steps.observed: All relevant tests pass across both files

steps.action: Read test source for ambiguity detection in findSessionForReply
steps.expected: When reply doesn't contain either task label with multiple sessions, returns null (triggering disambiguation)
steps.observed: Test 'should return null when reply is ambiguous across multiple sessions (VAL-SESSION-004)' — creates 2 sessions (get food, call mom), sends '10pm' which contains neither label, asserts result is null

steps.action: Read disambiguation-followup.test.ts for full disambiguation flow
steps.expected: Integration tests verify: ambiguous input triggers disambiguation prompt listing tasks, then user reply resolves it
steps.observed: Full flow tested: (1) 'cancel that' with 2 tasks → sendMessage called with 'which task' + both task names + numbered list. (2) User replies '1' → correct task canceled. (3) Also tested for edit ('make that 9') and done flows. (4) Unrecognized reply re-asks disambiguation. (5) Number out of range re-asks.

relevantTests: findSessionForReply > should return null when reply is ambiguous across multiple sessions (VAL-SESSION-004) ✓
findSessionForReply > should return null when reply matches multiple session labels ✓
cancel disambiguation > Step 1: 'cancel that' with 2 tasks triggers disambiguation ✓
edit disambiguation > Step 1: 'make that 9' with 2 tasks triggers disambiguation ✓
done disambiguation > Step 1: 'done' with multiple active tasks triggers disambiguation ✓
still ambiguous reply > unrecognized reply re-asks with task list ✓
still ambiguous reply > number out of range re-asks disambiguation ✓

steps.expected: Tests for expireOldSessions pass, verifying 24-hour staleness cleanup

steps.action: Read test source for expireOldSessions tests
steps.expected: Tests verify: sessions older than maxAgeHours are resolved, default is 24 hours, custom age supported
steps.observed: Tests verify: (1) Sessions inactive for >maxAgeHours are resolved — update called with resolved_at, filtered by user_id, resolved_at IS null, updated_at < cutoff. (2) Default 24 hours — cutoff calculated as now - 24*60*60*1000ms. (3) Returns 0 when no expired sessions. (4) Error handling works. (5) Custom maxAgeHours (12) uses correct cutoff.

steps.action: Verify lifecycle integration test for staleness
steps.expected: Integration test confirms stale sessions identified correctly
steps.observed: Test 'should correctly identify stale sessions for expiry' calls expireOldSessions with 24h, verifies 2 old sessions expired

relevantTests: expireOldSessions (VAL-EDGE-003) > should resolve sessions inactive for more than maxAgeHours ✓
expireOldSessions (VAL-EDGE-003) > should default to 24 hours when maxAgeHours is not provided ✓
expireOldSessions (VAL-EDGE-003) > should return 0 when no sessions are expired ✓
expireOldSessions (VAL-EDGE-003) > should throw an error when Supabase returns an error ✓
expireOldSessions (VAL-EDGE-003) > should use custom maxAgeHours when provided ✓
Session lifecycle integration > should correctly identify stale sessions for expiry ✓

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed. All session management behaviors (follow-up routing, multi-session coexistence, task name in prompts, disambiguation triggers, staleness cleanup) are verified by meaningful passing tests across sessions.test.ts (31 tests) and disambiguation-followup.test.ts (31 tests), totaling 62 tests all passing.
